## The Code: Scaled Dot-Product Attention in Python

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="transpose"}
```

`transpose` swaps rows and columns so attention can compare every query row with every key row.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="matrix_add"}
```

`matrix_add` adds two matrices element by element.
Attention uses it to add the causal mask to the score matrix.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="matrix_scale"}
```

`matrix_scale` multiplies every entry by the same scalar.
Attention uses this for the $1/\sqrt{d_k}$ scaling factor.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="matrix_multiply"}
```

`matrix_multiply` computes the query-key score matrix and later the weighted sum of values.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="hstack"}
```

`hstack` concatenates matrices column-wise.
It is used later by multi-head attention after each head has produced its own output.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="softmax_vector"}
```

`softmax` converts a vector of raw scores into a probability distribution.
It subtracts the maximum value before exponentiating to prevent overflow.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="softmax_rows"}
```

`softmax_rows` applies the same conversion independently to every row of the attention score matrix.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="causal_mask"}
```

`causal_mask` fills the upper triangle with $-10^9$ so future tokens cannot be attended to.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="sdpa"}
```

`scaled_dot_product_attention` is a direct translation of the attention formula: scores, mask, softmax, weighted sum.
`self_attention` first projects the same input matrix into queries, keys, and values, then calls SDPA.

Run with `python3 src/python/chapter_demos.py`.
